services.name: Azure Container Apps
services.purpose: Hosts the WeTTY web terminal container (Express + Socket.IO on port 3000)

services.name: Azure Container Apps Environment
services.purpose: Managed environment for Container Apps — provides ingress, networking, and logging integration

services.name: Azure Container Registry
services.purpose: Stores the WeTTY Docker image built from containers/wetty/Dockerfile.azure

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Secrets management — stores SSH credentials and any runtime secrets

services.name: Log Analytics Workspace
services.purpose: Centralized log collection for Container Apps and Application Insights

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: APM and telemetry — WeTTY exposes /metrics via prom-client; App Insights provides live metrics and alerts

breakdown.note: Free grant: 180K vCPU-sec + 360K GiB-sec/sub/month. Scale-to-zero = $0 when idle.
First 5 GB/month free; typical app logs stay within free tier
Billed via Log Analytics; covered by 5 GB free tier

costEstimate.disclaimer: Estimate based on Azure Retail Prices API (live). Actual costs depend on usage. Monitor at portal.azure.com/costmanagement.

rejectedAlternatives.reason: User explicitly chose Container Apps. App Service container mode would also work but lacks native scale-to-zero and event-driven scaling.
node-pty and gc-stats require node-gyp. F1/Free tier is not viable. Container deployment preferred over startup-install pattern.
Container Apps provides built-in HTTPS ingress with automatic TLS termination — a separate nginx container is unnecessary overhead.

assumptions: Container Apps scale-to-zero during idle — $0 when no active connections
Log Analytics ingestion <5 GB/month — covered by free tier
SSH target host (SSHHOST) must be configured via environment variable at deploy time
Dockerfile.azure (without BuildKit syntax) will be generated at scaffold time for ACR compatibility
Managed Identity used for Container App → ACR pull (no registry credentials stored)

deployStrategy.reason: WeTTY has a Dockerfile with a Node.js/Express backend and native modules (node-pty, gc-stats). Container-only deploy via ACR is the only viable path.

instrumentation.reason: WeTTY exposes /metrics via prom-client. Application Insights provides live metrics, distributed tracing, and alerts with minimal config.

postDeployRecommendations.title: Configure a dedicated SSH target
postDeployRecommendations.reason: WeTTY defaults SSHHOST to localhost. In production, set the SSHHOST env var to point to a dedicated SSH server (VM or container). Without this, the terminal connects to itself which may not be useful.

postDeployRecommendations.title: Enable TLS at the ingress
postDeployRecommendations.reason: Container Apps provides automatic HTTPS ingress. No additional configuration needed — TLS terminates at the ingress layer.

postDeployRecommendations.title: Upgrade to Dedicated plan for sustained load
postDeployRecommendations.reason: Consumption plan scales to zero, which introduces cold start latency for terminal sessions. For always-on usage, a Dedicated plan eliminates cold starts.
